scope: Reviewed public evidence profile for crude-tanker cycle interpretation.
cycle_conclusion: Use oil-trade disruption and ton-mile evidence as confirmation or contradiction for tanker price signals.
current_phase_hypothesis: Transition watch when oil-market disruption rises but freight and relative-price evidence diverge.
valuation_context: Valuation proxies remain incomplete without true vessel-rate and subsector multiple histories.
market_cycle_watch: Watch oil trade, inventories, route disruption, fleet supply, relative price, and valuation proxy together.
why_now: Public maritime and oil-market evidence can clarify whether tanker strength is fundamental or sentiment-led.
key_debates: Trade disruption versus weak oil demand; fleet tightness versus new supply.
catalysts: OPEC+ decisions, Hormuz/Red Sea route risk, inventory draws, freight-rate inflections.
risks: False confirmation from broad energy rallies without tanker-specific trade-flow support.

scope: Reviewed public evidence profile for product and chemical tanker cycle interpretation.
cycle_conclusion: Use refining activity, product inventories, and trade-flow dislocation before treating crude-price moves as tanker-cycle evidence.
current_phase_hypothesis: Transition watch when refinery dislocation improves but product-demand or inventory evidence weakens.
valuation_context: Valuation proxies are incomplete without product tanker freight and true Oslo subsector multiples.
market_cycle_watch: Watch refining margins, product inventories, oil trade, route disruption, relative price, and valuation proxy.
why_now: Product tanker cycles can diverge from crude tanker cycles when refinery geography changes.
key_debates: Refinery disruption versus demand destruction; ton-mile gains versus fleet normalization.
catalysts: Refinery outages, sanctions, product inventory turns, route disruption, seasonal demand.
risks: False confirmation from crude-price strength alone.

scope: Reviewed public evidence profile for dry-bulk cycle interpretation.
cycle_conclusion: Use dry-bulk freight direction and China/commodity evidence as confirmation or contradiction for industrial commodity proxy signals.
current_phase_hypothesis: Recovery candidate only when freight, China proxies, metals, and relative price confirm each other.
valuation_context: Valuation proxies are incomplete without true freight-linked earnings and subsector multiples.
market_cycle_watch: Watch dry-bulk freight, copper, aluminum, China CLI, G20 CLI, relative price, and valuation proxy.
why_now: UNCTAD freight evidence helps separate commodity-led optimism from actual shipping-cycle improvement.
key_debates: China demand versus vessel supply; commodity-price rallies versus freight softness.
catalysts: China stimulus, steel output, grain flows, fleet orderbook, freight-rate inflections.
risks: False recovery calls when metals rally but freight remains weak.

scope: Reviewed public evidence profile for LNG/LPG shipping cycle interpretation.
cycle_conclusion: Use gas balances, LNG supply disruption, route risk, and fleet delivery timing as core cycle evidence.
current_phase_hypothesis: Transition or exit-risk watch when gas-price volatility rises faster than shipping fundamentals improve.
valuation_context: Valuation proxies are incomplete without vessel-rate and contract-cover histories.
market_cycle_watch: Watch gas prices, LNG supply, Asian/European demand, route risk, fleet deliveries, relative price, and valuation proxy.
why_now: Public IEA gas evidence directly improves gas-shipping cycle interpretation.
key_debates: Supply shock versus demand destruction; energy security demand versus fleet additions.
catalysts: Gas market reports, LNG outage updates, Asian demand, European storage, fleet delivery schedule.
risks: False confirmation from energy-security narratives without shipping-rate support.

scope: Reviewed public evidence profile for offshore vessels and drilling cycle interpretation.
cycle_conclusion: Use oil balances, offshore capex, dayrates, utilization, and backlog quality together before calling continuation.
current_phase_hypothesis: Continuation candidate when oil balances support capex and rig/vessel supply stays tight; exit risk when oil volatility cuts budgets.
valuation_context: Valuation proxies are incomplete without true dayrate, backlog, and subsector multiple histories.
market_cycle_watch: Watch Brent, oil curve pressure, IEA oil balances, utilization, dayrates, backlog, relative price, and valuation proxy.
why_now: Oil-market volatility affects E&P budget confidence and offshore tender timing.
key_debates: Capex expansion versus budget discipline; high utilization versus margin conversion.
catalysts: E&P budgets, tender awards, utilization reports, dayrate inflections, backlog updates.
risks: False confirmation from oil price alone.

scope: Reviewed public evidence profile for oil and gas E&P cycle interpretation.
cycle_conclusion: Use commodity cash-flow support and geopolitical exit risk together rather than treating oil-price strength as uniformly positive.
current_phase_hypothesis: Late-cycle or exit-risk watch when energy-price shocks support near-term cash flow but raise demand and policy risk.
valuation_context: Valuation proxies are incomplete without true reserve, decline-rate, and Oslo E&P multiple histories.
market_cycle_watch: Watch Brent, WTI, gas prices, inventories, policy risk, NOK, relative price, and valuation proxy.
why_now: The 2026 commodity-price shock makes cash-flow and risk-regime separation important.
key_debates: Cash return versus reinvestment; geopolitical premium versus demand destruction.
catalysts: Oil-market reports, gas-market reports, policy/tax updates, curve shifts.
risks: False confidence from headline commodity prices without reserve and policy context.

scope: Reviewed public evidence profile for oil-services cycle interpretation.
cycle_conclusion: Use public oil-market anchors before treating order intake, backlog, and margin claims as cycle evidence.
current_phase_hypothesis: Recovery confirmation requires order/backlog improvement plus supportive oil balances and stable funding conditions.
valuation_context: Valuation proxies are incomplete without backlog quality, margin, and true subsector multiple histories.
market_cycle_watch: Watch oil balances, EIA data, offshore capex, order intake, backlog quality, rates, relative price, and valuation proxy.
why_now: Public oil-market anchors help avoid unreviewed backlog narratives driving conclusions.
key_debates: Order growth versus margin conversion; capex recovery versus cost inflation.
catalysts: Quarterly backlog commentary, E&P capex budgets, tender awards, oil balance shifts.
risks: False confirmation from unsourced order-intake claims.

scope: Reviewed public evidence profile for seafood and aquaculture cycle interpretation.
cycle_conclusion: Use export value, volume, price realization, biological risk, feed costs, regulation, and FX together.
current_phase_hypothesis: Transition watch when export value rises on volume while price realization weakens.
valuation_context: Valuation proxies are incomplete without salmon-price, biomass, and true subsector multiple histories.
market_cycle_watch: Watch salmon exports, price/volume mix, feed costs, disease, regulation, NOK/EUR, and relative price.
why_now: 2025 export data show why headline seafood value can hide price/volume contradiction.
key_debates: Volume growth versus price weakness; biological risk versus demand resilience.
catalysts: Export releases, salmon-price updates, biomass reports, regulatory changes.
risks: False confidence from record export value without price realization.

scope: Reviewed public evidence profile for metals and aluminum cycle interpretation.
cycle_conclusion: Use commodity breadth, energy costs, China demand, and industrial growth proxies together.
current_phase_hypothesis: Recovery candidate only when metals prices, energy-cost pressure, China evidence, and relative price confirm.
valuation_context: Valuation proxies are incomplete without smelter margin and true subsector multiple histories.
market_cycle_watch: Watch aluminum, copper, energy prices, China CLI, G20 CLI, relative price, and valuation proxy.
why_now: World Bank commodity evidence helps separate broad commodity shocks from metals-specific recovery.
key_debates: Energy-cost pressure versus metal-price support; China demand versus supply additions.
catalysts: Commodity outlooks, China data, energy-price changes, production curtailments.
risks: False confirmation from metal-price strength caused mainly by supply shocks.

scope: Reviewed public evidence profile for renewables cycle interpretation.
cycle_conclusion: Use rates, project returns, policy, manufacturing trends, and sector financial health as core evidence.
current_phase_hypothesis: Recovery candidate when rates ease and project economics improve; exit risk when policy or financing deteriorates.
valuation_context: Valuation proxies are incomplete without project-return and true subsector multiple histories.
market_cycle_watch: Watch rates pressure, policy changes, power prices, supply chains, copper, relative price, and valuation proxy.
why_now: IEA sector evidence ties the renewables read to policy, manufacturing, and financial health.
key_debates: Rate relief versus policy risk; growth demand versus poor returns.
catalysts: Central-bank paths, auctions, power-price data, supply-chain updates.
risks: False confirmation from green-equity sentiment alone.

scope: Reviewed public evidence profile for Norwegian bank cycle interpretation.
cycle_conclusion: Use rates, credit losses, funding spreads, housing, and CRE exposure together.
current_phase_hypothesis: Late-cycle or deterioration watch when CRE vulnerability rises while rates stay restrictive.
valuation_context: Valuation proxies are incomplete without true bank valuation, credit-loss, and funding-spread histories.
market_cycle_watch: Watch Norges Bank rates, CPI, CRE exposure, housing, credit losses, funding stress, and relative price.
why_now: Norges Bank financial-stability evidence links bank confidence to CRE credit risk.
key_debates: Higher rates supporting NIM versus credit losses; CRE stress versus capital buffers.
catalysts: Policy meetings, financial-stability reports, credit-loss updates, housing data.
risks: False confidence from NIM strength without credit-cycle evidence.

scope: Reviewed public evidence profile for real-estate cycle interpretation.
cycle_conclusion: Use rates, refinancing pressure, rents, asset values, and CRE selling-price evidence together.
current_phase_hypothesis: Transition watch when asset prices flatten but refinancing pressure remains high.
valuation_context: Valuation proxies are incomplete without true property valuation, debt maturity, and subsector multiple histories.
market_cycle_watch: Watch policy rate path, inflation, funding spreads, CRE selling prices, rents, relative price, and valuation proxy.
why_now: Norges Bank CRE evidence improves refinancing and asset-value risk interpretation.
key_debates: Rate relief versus refinancing wall; stable rents versus asset-value pressure.
catalysts: Policy-rate changes, property-price data, refinancing updates, vacancy/rent data.
risks: False recovery calls from rate-cut hopes without asset-value confirmation.

scope: Reviewed public evidence profile for industrial and tech exporter cycle interpretation.
cycle_conclusion: Use global growth, financial conditions, tech capex, FX, and order intake together.
current_phase_hypothesis: Continuation candidate only when macro confirmation, tech demand, and relative price align.
valuation_context: Valuation proxies are incomplete without true order-book, margin, and subsector multiple histories.
market_cycle_watch: Watch G20 CLI, US CLI, Nasdaq proxy, USD/NOK, EUR/NOK, financial conditions, and relative price.
why_now: IMF global-risk evidence helps separate exporter-cycle demand from local price momentum.
key_debates: Tech capex versus macro slowdown; weak NOK support versus global demand risk.
catalysts: PMI/CLI changes, order intake, capex commentary, FX moves.
risks: False confirmation from currency translation without demand evidence.
